title: Efficient Longest Increasing Subsequence with Incorrect Handling of Zero Elements

statement.markdown: Given an array of integers, find the length of the longest increasing subsequence. This problem can be solved using dynamic programming. However, the current implementation does not handle the case when the input array contains zero elements correctly.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be an array of integers.
statement.outputFormat: The output should be the length of the longest increasing subsequence.
statement.notes: The input array can contain negative numbers and zero.

constraints.description: The length of the input array.

hints.text: The current implementation uses dynamic programming to build up the longest increasing subsequence.
However, it does not handle the case when the input array contains zero elements correctly.

editorial.title: Efficient Longest Increasing Subsequence with Correct Handling of Zero Elements
editorial.intuition: The key insight is to handle the case when the input array contains zero elements correctly by initializing the dp array with zeros.
editorial.bruteForce: The brute force approach is to use a dynamic programming table to store the length of the longest increasing subsequence ending at each position.
editorial.betterSolution: The better solution is to use a dynamic programming table to store the length of the longest increasing subsequence ending at each position and handle the case when the input array contains zero elements correctly.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use a dynamic programming table to store the length of the longest increasing subsequence ending at each position and handle the case when the input array contains zero elements correctly by initializing the dp array with zeros.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness is by induction on the length of the input array.
editorial.dryRun: The dry run example is to find the length of the longest increasing subsequence in the input array [10, 22, 9, 33, 21, 50, 41, 60, 80].
commonMistakes: Not handling the case when the input array contains zero elements correctly.
Not initializing the dp array with zeros.
